title: Add PodDisruptionBudget to Keystone reconcileDeployment sub-reconciler
description:
**Size:** 🔧 small
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** high
**Source:** Proposed for 'Implement PodDisruptionBudget
  - In reconcileDeplo'

Implement PDB support in the Keystone operator:

1. **`buildPodDisruptionBudget()`** in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go`: Construct `policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget` named `{keystone.Name}-api`, using `commonLabels()` on ObjectMeta and `selectorLabels()` on `Spec.Selector.MatchLabels`. Use `minAvailable: 1` when `replicas > 1`, `maxUnavailable: 1` when `replicas == 1` to prevent drain deadlocks.

2. **`EnsurePDB()`** in `internal/common/deployment/deployment.go`: Reusable get-or-create/update helper following the existing `EnsureDeployment`/`EnsureService` contract — set owner reference via `SetControllerReference` on create, patch spec on update, return error only (no readiness bool). Use symmetric DeepCopy-then-normalize before spec comparison per codebase patterns.

3. **RBAC marker** on `Reconcile()` in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go`: Add `+kubebuilder:rbac:groups=policy,resources=poddisruptionbudgets,verbs=get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete` alongside existing markers.

4. **`SetupWithManager` watch** in `keystone_controller.go`: Add `Owns(&policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget{})` so PDB changes trigger reconciliation.

5. **Import** `policyv1 "k8s.io/api/policy/v1"` in both controller and deployment helper files.

6. **Call site** in `reconcileDeployment`: Invoke `buildPodDisruptionBudget()` + `EnsurePDB()` after `EnsureService`, before setting the `DeploymentReady` condition.

7. **Tests** in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go`: Add `policyv1` to `deployTestScheme()`. Test PDB creation (owner ref, labels, selector matches Deployment pod selector), correct `minAvailable`/`maxUnavailable` for replica counts >1 and ==1, and update-on-replica-change behavior. Follow existing Gomega BDD patterns with `NewGomegaWithT`.

**Rationale:** Keystone currently has no PodDisruptionBudget. During voluntary disruptions (kubectl drain, node upgrades, cluster maintenance), all Keystone API pods can be evicted simultaneously, causing a complete API outage. With a PDB, Kubernetes guarantees at least one pod remains available during rolling disruptions, providing zero-downtime resilience for the identity service that all other OpenStack components depend on.

**Affected Areas:**
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go
- internal/common/deployment/deployment.go
elaboration:
**Size:** 🔧 small
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** high

## Summary

Add a `PodDisruptionBudget` to the Keystone operator's `reconcileDeployment` sub-reconciler. The PDB ensures that during voluntary disruptions (node drain, cluster upgrades), at least one Keystone API pod remains available when running multiple replicas. The strategy switches between `minAvailable: 1` (replicas > 1) and `maxUnavailable: 1` (replicas == 1) to prevent drain deadlocks with single-replica deployments.

## Scope

**Included:**
- `buildPodDisruptionBudget()` function in `reconcile_deployment.go` following existing `buildKeystoneDeployment`/`buildKeystoneService` pattern
- `EnsurePDB()` helper in `internal/common/deployment/deployment.go` following `EnsureDeployment`/`EnsureService` contract (get-or-create, patch spec on update, set owner reference)
- PDB creation/update call within `reconcileDeployment` — after `EnsureService`, before condition-setting (lines ~96-100 in current code)
- RBAC marker `+kubebuilder:rbac:groups=policy,resources=poddisruptionbudgets,verbs=get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete` on `Reconcile()` (alongside existing markers at `keystone_controller.go:47-56`)
- `Owns(&policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget{})` in `SetupWithManager` (alongside existing Owns for Deployment, Service, ConfigMap, Job, CronJob at `keystone_controller.go:145-162`)
- Owner references, `commonLabels()`, `selectorLabels()` — consistent with Deployment/Service usage
- `policyv1 "k8s.io/api/policy/v1"` import (not yet present in codebase; k8s v0.35.3 supports policy/v1 stable)
- Unit tests following `reconcile_deployment_test.go` patterns (Gomega BDD, fake client, `deployTestScheme()` helper)
- `policyv1` scheme registration in `deployTestScheme()`

**Excluded:**
- User-configurable PDB spec fields in `KeystoneSpec` (YAGNI — sensible defaults suffice; can be added later)
- Separate sub-reconciler or status condition for PDB (too small to warrant its own phase; natural part of deployment readiness)
- PDB for CronJob pods (fernet/credential rotation are short-lived batch jobs, PDB is inappropriate)
- Anti-affinity rules (separate concern, not requested)

## Visualization

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    RD["reconcileDeployment"]
    RD --> BD["buildKeystoneDeployment"]
    BD --> ED["EnsureDeployment"]
    ED --> RDY{"Deployment Ready?"}
    RDY -->|No| RQ["Requeue 10s"]
    RDY -->|Yes| BS["buildKeystoneService"]
    BS --> ES["EnsureService"]
    ES --> BP["buildPodDisruptionBudget (NEW)"]
    BP --> EP["EnsurePDB (NEW)"]
    EP --> CHK{"Replicas > 1?"}
    CHK -->|Yes| MA["minAvailable: 1"]
    CHK -->|No| MU["maxUnavailable: 1"]
    EP --> SC["Set DeploymentReady Condition"]
```

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph Owned["Keystone-Owned Resources"]
        DEP["Deployment"]
        SVC["Service"]
        CM["ConfigMap"]
        JOB["Job"]
        CRON["CronJob"]
        PDB["PDB (NEW)"]
    end
    KS["Keystone CR"] -->|ownerRef| DEP
    KS -->|ownerRef| SVC
    KS -->|ownerRef| CM
    KS -->|ownerRef| JOB
    KS -->|ownerRef| CRON
    KS -->|ownerRef| PDB
    PDB -.->|selectorLabels| DEP
```

## Key Components

- **`buildPodDisruptionBudget()`** in `reconcile_deployment.go`: Constructs `policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget` with `commonLabels()` on ObjectMeta, `selectorLabels()` on `Spec.Selector.MatchLabels`. Chooses `minAvailable: 1` when `replicas > 1`, `maxUnavailable: 1` when `replicas == 1`. Resource named `{keystone.Name}-api` (matching Deployment/Service naming convention).
- **`EnsurePDB()`** in `internal/common/deployment/deployment.go`: Reusable get-or-create/update helper following `EnsureDeployment`/`EnsureService` contract. Sets owner reference on create, patches spec on update. Returns error (no readiness concept for PDB).
- **RBAC marker** on `Reconcile()` in `keystone_controller.go`: `+kubebuilder:rbac:groups=policy,resources=poddisruptionbudgets,verbs=get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete`.
- **`SetupWithManager` watch** in `keystone_controller.go`: `Owns(&policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget{})` so PDB changes trigger reconciliation.
- **Tests** in `reconcile_deployment_test.go`: PDB creation assertions (owner ref, labels, selector matches Deployment pod selector), correct `minAvailable`/`maxUnavailable` based on replica count (test both >1 and ==1 paths), update-on-replica-change test, `policyv1` added to `deployTestScheme()`.
